params.name: Caesar Statue
params.description:
On Feb 24, 2007 AD, a marble bust of Caesar was discovered in the Rhone of France.
Julius Caesar was an outstanding military commander and statesman in the late period of the Roman Republic and is regarded as the founder of the Roman Empire. This sculpture is a bust created on a life-sized scale. The sculpture conveys the anxiety and pressure he faced, with his brows furrowed, and a sad expression on his face, as if he was so worried about the future.
